author: Jeremy Evans
message:
Fix instance_variables_to_inspect place

Previously, it operating in the Roda scope, not in Rodauth scope.
While it's a good idea in Roda scope (at least when the Roda
shape_friendly plugin is used), that change should be made in Roda.
We should only make the change for Rodauth scope, so move this to
Base. Additionally, add a spec for it (the spec is actually what
caught this issue), and move the nocov markers to only contain the
if.

author: Janko Marohnić
message: Exclude nil instance variables from `#inspect` output on Ruby 4+

author: Jeremy Evans
message:
Work with Roda shape_friendly plugin

This sets the SCOPE_INSTANCE_VARIABLES plugin constant so that
the Roda scope instance variables that rodauth uses are set to
nil on initialization.

author: Jeremy Evans
message:
Add Rodauth::OPTS empty frozen hash

This helps avoid default hash allocations. The default hashes
shouldn't be modified, and this helps detect cases where they are.

Additionally, places that use {}.freeze switch to using OPTS.
On Ruby 3.4+, these places don't allocate, but this helps avoid
allocations on earlier versions.

author: Jeremy Evans
message:
Remove unnecessary to_sym calls

This hasn't been needed since Ruby 1.8 support was dropped.

author: Jeremy Evans
message:
Add transaction_opts configuration method for defining transaction options

The expected use case is for setting transaction isolation levels,
but it can be used for other things.

author: Jeremy Evans
message:
Warn if feature configuration methods define methods that the feature does not implement

In this case, it is likely that the feature configuration methods do
not operate as expected. If a feature really needs a configuration
method to define a method that it does not itself define, they can
use the new allowed_undefined_configuration_methods accessor. The
json feature uses it to allow configuration of the only_json? method,
which is defined in the base feature.

These warnings found all of the issues solved in the recent commits.

author: Jeremy Evans
message:
Make get_reset_password_key configuration method in reset_password feature work as expected

Previously, the method defined by the configuration method was not
called. This changes get_password_reset_key to call it.

author: Jeremy Evans
message:
Make unlock_account_key configuration method in lockout feature work as expected

Previously, the configuration method defined a method that was not
called. This changes the implementation to call the defined method.

author: Jeremy Evans
message:
Make sms_request_response configuration method in sms_codes feature work as expected

This was the opposite of convert_token_id, where the default method is
prefixed with an underscore, but the configuration method created
a method not prefixed by an underscore.

author: Jeremy Evans
message:
Make convert_token_id configuration method work as expected

Previously, convert_token_id was defined as an auth_private_method,
so it defined a method that started with an underscore, but the
actual method does not start with an underscore, so using the
configuration method defined an unused method.

author: Jeremy Evans
message: Bump version to 2.44.0

author: Jeremy Evans
message: Add nocov markers around code for older rack versions

author: Jeremy Evans
message: Update CHANGELOG

author: Yuki Fukuma
message: Conditionally require rack in base feature so Rack.release is defined

author: Jeremy Evans
message:
Clear other active sessions when first multifactor authentication method is added

When adding SMS, TOTP, or WebAuthn authentication, if two factor
authentication is not already setup, and the active_sessions feature
is in use, clear other active sessions. This prevents an active
session that was only single factor authenticated from being valid
when two factor authentication is enabled.

While the previous behavior is not considered a vulnerability, since
the session was fully authenticated when it was created, this seems
like a more secure default.

author: Jeremy Evans
message:
Use a shape-friendly approach on Ruby 3.2 with YJIT available

This changes Rodauth::Auth classes to use a shape-friendly approach
when YJIT is available. It assumes that if you care about performance,
you will be using YJIT if it is available, and using Ruby 3.2+.
On Ruby <3.2 or when YJIT is not available, the shape friendly
approach is not used, so the only instance variable set by default
is `@scope` (as it was before).

This works by tracking which instance variables are used by each
feature, using the new uses_instance_variables feature method.  When
creating a new Rodauth::Auth instance, the instance variables used by
all of the features in the configuration are set to nil (which is
fast if you are using YJIT). For instance variables set manually
inside a specific Rodauth configuration, the uses_instance_variables
method can also be used.

auth_cached_method previously used a non-shape-friendly approach,
because it checked whether an instance variable was defined.
cached_auth_method has been added to replace it. cached_auth_method
assumes that a nil instance variable value indicates the instance
variable has not been cached, and any non-nil value indicates it
has been cached.  This only works for cases where the cached value
cannot be nil. The sms_codes feature had a case using
auth_cached_method where the cached value could be nil. This
replaces it with manual caching, using false to indicate a cached
negative value. The use of false for negative caching is also used
in the http_basic_auth and otp features. In the internal_request
feature, the `@internal_request_return_value` could legitimately be
either nil or false, so that uses a
`@internal_request_return_value_set` instance variable to track
whether the `@internal_request_return_value` was set or not.

Cases previously using `if defined?(@iv)` now use `unless @iv.nil?`
or `if @iv`. There are no remaining uses of
`remove_instance_variable`, and the only remaining use of
`instance_variable_defined?` is in the soft-deprecated
`auth_cached_method` method.

In the json feature, json_request? previously returned an integer;
it now returns true or false.

author: Jeremy Evans
message: Add Add JRuby 10.1 to CI

author: Tomás Miguez
message: Fix typos in README.rdoc

author: Jeremy Evans
message:
Unbreak CI on Ruby 2.4/2.5

CI for these Ruby versions is currently broken due to a temporary
issue in Rack breaking support in these Ruby versions.

Only test rack-head on Ruby 4.0+.

Update CHANGELOG.

author: Jonas Mueller
message:
Add after_no_matching_login hook

In order to log failed login attempts, add a callback for a non-matching logins.

Usage:

    after_no_matching_login do
      logger.info "Failed login attempt for #{login_param_value}"
    end

author: Jeremy Evans
message: Fix web link to reset_password_verifies_account feature

author: Jeremy Evans
message: Bump version to 2.43.0

author: Jeremy Evans
message:
Use jsonb as json type in migrations on SQLite 3.45+

This doesn't actually matter except for documentation purposes,
because SQLite doesn't treat json and jsonb differently.

While here, have MySQL use the json type when testing in CI.

author: Jeremy Evans
message:
Mention not using foreign key for account_authentication_audit_logs.account_id

If you are allowing account deletions, you probably shouldn't have
a foreign key in the audit log, as it would mean you would either
have to delete the audit log records before deleting the account,
or you couldn't delete the account.

By default, Rodauth doesn't support account deletions. The
close_account feature does not delete accounts on close by default.
However, it can be configured to do so.

author: Jeremy Evans
message:
Add reset_password_verifies_account feature

This moves the related code from the reset_password feature to its own
feature, so that users only have to pay the cost for the feature if
they are using it.

Update the spec to provide 100% coverage.
